User Story: As a player I want to import the mcpack so textures apply in Bedrock
Expected Behaviour: Double-click or Open-in-Minecraft imports Cel_Band_Pack.mcpack without errors
Edge Cases: Duplicate import; wrong file type; MC < 1.21

User Story: As a player I want to import the mcpack so textures apply in Bedrock
Expected Behaviour: Pack appears in resource list as Cel Band Pack
Test Case Description: Verify pack name in MC resource picker

User Story: As a player I want global activation so all worlds use cel-band textures
Expected Behaviour: Settings → Global Resources → Cel Band Pack active
Test Case Description: Activate pack globally and load creative world

User Story: As a player I want per-world activation for selective use
Expected Behaviour: World edit → Resource Packs → add Cel Band Pack
Test Case Description: Activate pack on single world only

User Story: As a player I want four-band stone surfaces
Expected Behaviour: Calcite/stone/deepslate/bedrock show converted textures
Test Case Description: Verify calcite stone deepslate bedrock in creative

User Story: As a player I want four-band stone surfaces
Expected Behaviour: Walls placed via converter use stone texture (converted)
Test Case Description: Place wall tiles from sample build and verify stone texture

User Story: As a player I want relic palette on gold/emerald/copper
Expected Behaviour: Gold emerald copper blocks use converted relic art

User Story: As a player I want converted item icons
Expected Behaviour: Chorus fruit zombie head iron ingot prismarine trident use pack PNGs

User Story: As a player I want cel-band UI chrome
Expected Behaviour: Title and hotbar cap textures from pack apply if supported by Bedrock version

User Story: As a developer I want one command to rebuild distributables
Expected Behaviour: ./scripts/build_pack.sh assembles mcpack and refreshes download/

User Story: As a developer I want committed PNGs zipped into mcpack
Expected Behaviour: assemble_pack.py writes dist and download mcpack ≥8KB
Edge Cases: Empty pack dir; bad manifest JSON
Test Case Description: Dist mcpack exists and meets size floor

User Story: As a developer I want committed PNGs zipped into mcpack
Expected Behaviour: Zip contains manifest pack_icon and block textures

User Story: As CI I want post-build QA gate
Expected Behaviour: validate_pack.py checks structure branding version sample level

User Story: As a builder I want tile JSON → Bedrock placements
Expected Behaviour: convert_level.py maps 12 tile types to blocks with multi-block features
Edge Cases: Unknown tile type defaults to stone; invalid origin

User Story: As a builder I want tile JSON → Bedrock placements
Expected Behaviour: Wall tiles use stone (pack has converted texture)
Edge Cases: Previously mapped to cobblestone without texture

User Story: As a builder I want tile JSON → Bedrock placements
Expected Behaviour: Sand tile maps to sand block

User Story: As a builder I want tile JSON → Bedrock placements
Expected Behaviour: Invalid origin rejected

User Story: As a builder I want tile JSON → Bedrock placements
Expected Behaviour: Download bundle runs without --level flag

Expected Behaviour: Outputs setblock mcfunction and python list

Test Case Description: Paste sample build in creative at test origin
